item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak:I’m speaking at the Personal AI Summit in Los Angeles, California, USA, on Thursday, March 5, 2026.I’m giving the Ross Anderson Lecture at the University of Cambridge’s Churchill College at 5:30 PM GMT on Thursday, March 19, 2026.I’m speaking at RSAC 2026 in San Francisco, California, USA, on Wednesday, March 25, 2026.

item.title: One threat actor responsible for 83% of recent Ivanti RCE attacks
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Threat intelligence observations show that a single threat actor is responsible for most of the active exploitation of two critical vulnerabilities in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM), tracked as CVE-2026-21962 and CVE-2026-24061. [...]

item.title: Snail mail letters target Trezor and Ledger users in crypto-theft attacks
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: Threat actors are sending physical letters pretending to be from Trezor and Ledger, makers of cryptocurrency hardware wallets, to trick users into submitting recovery phrases in crypto theft attacks. [...]

item.title: Fake job recruiters hide malware in developer coding challenges
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A new variation of the fake recruiter campaign from North Korean threat actors is targeting JavaScript and Python developers with cryptocurrency-related tasks. [...]

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Do Squid Dream?
item.author: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Claude LLM artifacts abused to push Mac infostealers in ClickFix attack
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Threat actors are abusing Claude artifacts and Google Ads in ClickFix campaigns that deliver infostealer malware to macOS users searching for specific queries. [...]

item.title: Louis Vuitton, Dior, and Tiffany fined $25 million over data breaches
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: South Korea has fined luxury fashion brands Louis Vuitton, Christian Dior Couture, and Tiffany $25 million for failing to implement adequate security measures, which facilitated unauthorized access and the exposure of data belonging to more than 5.5 million customers. [...]

item.title: Google Ties Suspected Russian Actor to CANFAIL Malware Attacks on Ukrainian Orgs
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to attacks targeting Ukrainian organizations with malware known as CANFAIL. Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) described the hacking group as possibly affiliated with Russian intelligence services. The threat actor is assessed to have targeted defense, military, government, and energy organizations within the Ukrainian regional and

item.title: Google Links China, Iran, Russia, North Korea to Coordinated Defense Sector Cyber Operations
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Several state-sponsored actors, hacktivist entities, and criminal groups from China, Iran, North Korea, and Russia have trained their sights on the defense industrial base (DIB) sector, according to findings from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG). The tech giant's threat intelligence division said the adversarial targeting of the sector is centered around four key themes: striking defense

item.title: UAT-9921 Deploys VoidLink Malware to Target Technology and Financial Sectors
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A previously unknown threat actor tracked as UAT-9921 has been observed leveraging a new modular framework called VoidLink in its campaigns targeting the technology and financial services sectors, according to findings from Cisco Talos. "This threat actor seems to have been active since 2019, although they have not necessarily used VoidLink over the duration of their activity," researchers Nick

item.title: Turning IBM QRadar Alerts into Action with Criminal IP
item.author: Sponsored by Criminal IP
item.content:encoded: Criminal IP now integrates with IBM QRadar SIEM and SOAR to bring external IP-based threat intelligence directly into detection and response workflows. See how risk scoring and automated enrichment help SOC teams prioritize high-risk IPs and accelerate investigations without leaving QRadar. [...]

item.title: CISA flags critical Microsoft SCCM flaw as exploited in attacks
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: CISA ordered federal agencies on Thursday to secure their systems against a critical Microsoft Configuration Manager vulnerability patched in October 2024 and now exploited in attacks. [...]

item.title: Malicious Chrome Extensions Caught Stealing Business Data, Emails, and Browsing History
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious Google Chrome extension that's designed to steal data associated with Meta Business Suite and Facebook Business Manager. The extension, named CL Suite by @CLMasters (ID: jkphinfhmfkckkcnifhjiplhfoiefffl), is marketed as a way to scrape Meta Business Suite data, remove verification pop-ups, and generate two-factor authentication (2FA) codes.

item.title: npm’s Update to Harden Their Supply Chain, and Points to Consider
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: In December 2025, in response to the Sha1-Hulud incident, npm completed a major authentication overhaul intended to reduce supply-chain attacks. While the overhaul is a solid step forward, the changes don’t make npm projects immune from supply-chain attacks. npm is still susceptible to malware attacks – here’s what you need to know for a safer Node community. Let’s start with the original

item.title: Microsoft fixes bug that blocked Google Chrome from launching
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing its Family Safety parental control service to block Windows users from launching Google Chrome and other web browsers. [...]

item.title: Researchers Observe In-the-Wild Exploitation of BeyondTrust CVSS 9.9 Vulnerability
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Threat actors have started to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting BeyondTrust Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products, according to watchTowr. "Overnight we observed first in-the-wild exploitation of BeyondTrust across our global sensors," Ryan Dewhurst, head of threat intelligence at watchTowr, said in a post on X. "Attackers are abusing

item.title: Russia tries to block WhatsApp, Telegram in communication blockade
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: The Russian government is attempting to block WhatsApp in the country as its crackdown on communication platforms not under its control intensifies. [...]

item.title: Bitwarden introduces ‘Cupid Vault’ for secure password sharing
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Bitwarden has launched a new system called 'Cupid Vault' that allows users to safely share passwords with trusted email addresses. [...]

item.title: Critical BeyondTrust RCE flaw now exploited in attacks, patch now
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: A critical pre-authentication remote code execution vulnerability in BeyondTrust Remote Support and Privileged Remote Access appliances is now being exploited in attacks after a PoC was published online. [...]

item.title: Microsoft: New Windows LNK spoofing issues aren't vulnerabilities
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Today, at Wild West Hackin' Fest, security researcher Wietze Beukema disclosed multiple vulnerabilities in Windows LK shortcut files that allow attackers to deploy malicious payloads. [...]

item.title: Romania's oil pipeline operator Conpet confirms data stolen in attack
item.author: Ionut Ilascu
item.content:encoded: Romania's national oil pipeline operator, Conpet S.A., confirmed that the Qilin ransomware gang stole company data in an attack last week. [...]

item.title: Odido data breach exposes personal info of 6.2 million customers
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: Dutch telecommunications provider Odido is warning that it suffered a cyberattack that reportedly exposed the personal data of 6.2 million customers. [...]

item.title: Google Reports State-Backed Hackers Using Gemini AI for Recon and Attack Support
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Google on Thursday said it observed the North Korea-linked threat actor known as UNC2970 using its generative artificial intelligence (AI) model Gemini to conduct reconnaissance on its targets, as various hacking groups continue to weaponize the tool for accelerating various phases of the cyber attack life cycle, enabling information operations, and even conducting model extraction attacks. "The

item.title: WordPress plugin with 900k installs vulnerable to critical RCE flaw
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A critical vulnerability in the WPvivid Backup & Migration plugin for WordPress, installed on more than 900,000 websites, can be exploited to achieve remote code execution by uploading arbitrary files without authentication. [...]

item.title: Lazarus Campaign Plants Malicious Packages in npm and PyPI Ecosystems
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a fresh set of malicious packages across npm and the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository linked to a fake recruitment-themed campaign orchestrated by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group. The coordinated campaign has been codenamed graphalgo in reference to the first package published in the npm registry. It's assessed to be active since May 2025. "

item.title: AMOS infostealer targets macOS through a popular AI app
item.author: Sponsored by Flare
item.content:encoded: AMOS infostealer is targeting macOS users by abusing popular AI apps and extension marketplaces to harvest credentials. Flare examines how AMOS operates, spreads through AI-driven lures, and feeds the broader stealer-log cybercrime economy. [...]

item.title: Fake AI Chrome extensions with 300K users steal credentials, emails
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A set of 30 malicious Chrome extensions that have been installed by more than 300,000 users are masquerading as AI assistants to steal credentials, email content, and browsing information. [...]

item.title: 3D Printer Surveillance
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: New York is contemplating a bill that adds surveillance to 3D printers:New York’s 2026­2027 executive budget bill (S.9005 / A.10005) includes language that should alarm every maker, educator, and small manufacturer in the state. Buried in Part C is a provision requiring all 3D printers sold or delivered in New York to include “blocking technology.” This is defined as software or firmware that through a “firearms blueprint detection algorithm” and refuses to print anything it flags as a potential firearm or firearm component.I get the policy goals here, but the solution just won’t work. It’s the same problem as DRM: trying to prevent general-purpose computers from doing specific things. Cory Doctorow wrote about it in 2018 and—more generally—spoke about it in 2011.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: AI Prompt RCE, Claude 0-Click, RenEngine Loader, Auto 0-Days & 25+ Stories
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Threat activity this week shows one consistent signal — attackers are leaning harder on what already works. Instead of flashy new exploits, many operations are built around quiet misuse of trusted tools, familiar workflows, and overlooked exposures that sit in plain sight. Another shift is how access is gained versus how it’s used. Initial entry points are getting simpler, while post-compromise

item.title: The CTEM Divide: Why 84% of Security Programs Are Falling Behind
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A new 2026 market intelligence study of 128 enterprise security decision-makers (available here) reveals a stark divide forming between organizations – one that has nothing to do with budget size or industry and everything to do with a single framework decision. Organizations implementing Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) demonstrate 50% better attack surface visibility, 23-point

item.title: 83% of Ivanti EPMM Exploits Linked to Single IP on Bulletproof Hosting Infrastructure
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A significant chunk of the exploitation attempts targeting a newly disclosed security flaw in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) can be traced back to a single IP address on bulletproof hosting infrastructure offered by PROSPERO. Threat intelligence firm GreyNoise said it recorded 417 exploitation sessions from 8 unique source IP addresses between February 1 and 9, 2026. An estimated 346

item.title: Google says hackers are abusing Gemini AI for all attacks stages
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has published a new report warning about AI model extraction/distillation attacks, in which private-sector firms and researchers use legitimate API access to systematically probe models and replicate their logic and reasoning. [...]

item.title: Apple Fixes Exploited Zero-Day Affecting iOS, macOS, and Other Devices
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Apple on Wednesday released iOS, iPadOS, macOS Tahoe, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS updates to address a zero-day flaw that it said has been exploited in sophisticated cyber attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20700 (CVSS score: 7.8), has been described as a memory corruption issue in dyld, Apple's Dynamic Link Editor. Successful exploitation of the vulnerability could allow an

item.title: Apple fixes zero-day flaw used in 'extremely sophisticated' attacks
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: Apple has released security updates to fix a zero-day vulnerability that was exploited in an "extremely sophisticated attack" targeting specific individuals. [...]

item.title: Windows 11 Notepad flaw let files execute silently via Markdown links
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has fixed a "remote code execution" vulnerability in Windows 11 Notepad that allowed attackers to execute local or remote programs by tricking users into clicking specially crafted Markdown links, without displaying any Windows security warnings. [...]

item.title: Microsoft Store Outlook add-in hijacked to steal 4,000 Microsoft accounts
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: The AgreeTo add-in for Outlook has been hijacked and turned into a phishing kit that stole more than 4,000 Microsoft account credentials. [...]
